subject: Gaming
title: Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment
description: A quick, practical guide to Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment.

hook.text: Resident Evil 4 secretly watches how you play. If you're dying too much, it makes enemies weaker.

buildup.text: Dynamic difficulty adjustment—DDA—modifies a game's challenge in real-time based on player performance.

discovery.text: Left 4 Dead's AI Director spawns more zombies when you're doing well and fewer when you're struggling.

twist.text: Players hate DDA when they discover it. Knowing the game is going easy on you feels patronizing.

climax.text: The best DDA is invisible. If players notice the adjustment, the illusion of achievement is broken.

punchline.text: The game adjusts without telling you. Discovery ruins the magic.

quiz.question: Why do players often dislike dynamic difficulty adjustment?
options: Knowing the game is going easy on them feels patronizing
